title: lamium maculatum lusus lacteum
intro: Lamium maculatum lusus lacteum, also known as spotted lamium or white spotted lamium, is a cultivar of the spotted deadnettle plant, characterized by its unique white spots on the leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to relieve digestive issues, reduce pain and swelling, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Lamium maculatum lusus lacteum include reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
